Variant Library · v0.3.0
MODDABLE CHESS
Complete Variant Library

Every chess variant, one engine

64Variants
2–6Players
10+Age

Published by Moddable Games · v0.3.0

Variant Library

Moddable Chess includes 64 playable variants spanning classic rules, asymmetric battles, hidden information, expanded boards, and alternative win conditions. Each variant has its own complete rules page.

Standard Board (8×8)

  • Standard Chess — Classic FIDE rules
  • Andernach Chess — Capturing piece changes colour
  • Atomic — Captures explode adjacent pieces
  • Benedict Chess — Attacked enemies convert colour
  • Berserk Chess — Check grants a bonus move
  • Checkless Chess — No check unless it is checkmate
  • Dark Chess — Only see squares your pieces occupy
  • Dice Chess — Die roll constrains piece type
  • Duck Chess — Place a blocker duck after each move
  • Extinction Chess — Lose when any piece type is eliminated
  • Fischer Random — 960 randomised starting positions
  • Fog of War — Only see squares your pieces attack
  • Grid Chess — Moves must cross a 2×2 grid line
  • Horde Chess — 36 pawns vs a normal army
  • King of the Hill — King reaches centre = instant win
  • Madrasi Chess — Same-type opposing pieces paralyse each other
  • Maharaja & Sepoys — One super-piece vs a full army
  • Marseillais — Two moves per turn
  • No Castling — Standard chess, castling disabled
  • No Retreat — Pieces cannot move backward
  • Patrol Chess — Capture only when defended by a friendly piece
  • Racing Kings — No checks allowed, race King to rank 8
  • Rifle Chess — Capturing pieces stay on their square
  • Torpedo Chess — Pawns can always double-move
  • Weak! — Weakest piece type must move first

Multi-Move Variants

  • Monster Chess — White gets 2 moves, Black gets 1
  • Progressive — Escalating moves per turn (1, 2, 3, 4…)

Alternative Win Conditions

  • Antichess — Forced captures, lose all pieces to win
  • Codrus — Lose your King to win, no check concept
  • Five-Check — Five checks to win
  • Giveaway — Forced captures, lose all pieces to win (stalemate = loss)
  • Omnicide — Lose all pieces to win, captures NOT forced
  • Single-Check — One check wins instantly
  • Stalemate Wins — Stalemating your opponent wins
  • Suicide Chess — Forced captures, lose all pieces to win (stalemate = draw)
  • Three-Check — Deliver three checks to win

Piece Replacement Variants

  • Almost Chess — One queen replaced by Chancellor (R+N)
  • Amazon Chess — Queens replaced by Amazons (Q+N)
  • Chigorin — White has 4 knights instead of bishops
  • Knightmate — Knight is royal, King moves like a knight

Starting Position Variants

  • Endgame Chess — Only pawns and Kings from the start
  • Pawns Only — Only pawns, first promotion wins
  • Peasants’ Revolt — King + pawns vs King + knights
  • Upside-Down — Pieces start on opponent’s rank

Regional Variant

  • Makpong — Thai variant, King cannot move out of check

Expanded Boards

  • Capablanca Chess — 10×8 with Archbishop + Chancellor
  • Courier Chess — 12×8, medieval variant from 1202
  • Grand Chess — 10×10, same new pieces, bigger stage

Small Boards

  • Breakthrough — 7×7, pawns only, first to far rank wins
  • Diana Chess — 6×6, no queens or knights
  • Half Chess — 8×4, armies start adjacent
  • Los Alamos — 6×6, no bishops, first computer chess (1956)
  • Minichess — 5×5, full piece types on tiny board
  • Petty Chess — 5×6, all piece types, single copies
